steps.title: Make two points for the line
steps.content: The line passes through $(0,14)$. Its slope is $\frac{1}{9}$, so moving $9$ units right raises $y$ by $1$. That gives a second point, $(9,15)$.

steps.title: Use the points directly
steps.content: Use the table points directly. From $(0,14)$ to $(9,15)$, the slope is $m=\frac{15-14}{9-0}=\frac{1}{9}.$ Substituting a point into $y=mx+b$ gives $b=14$. That matches Choice D — $y=\frac{1}{9}x+14$.
